title: Piqueria peruviana
intro: Piqueria peruviana is a plant species native to the Andean region, belonging to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a type of flowering daisy. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various ailments including wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal issues. Bioactive constituents of Piqueria peruviana include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, this plant is used to prepare infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed as herbal teas, capsules, or topical creams to harness its medicinal benefits.
taxonomy: Piqueria peruviana is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on its shared characteristics and genetic relationships with other species within the family. Piqueria peruviana is further classified within the genus Piqueria, which is a group of flowering plants native to South America. The genus Piqueria is part of the tribe Heliantheae, a large and diverse group of plants. The classification of Piqueria peruviana provides a framework for understanding its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
uses: Piqueria peruviana, also known as the Peruvian Piqueria, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. Its roots and leaves are used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Additionally, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating conditions like arthritis. In some regions, it is also used to treat digestive issues and reduce fever. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of its medicinal uses.

constituents: Piqueria peruviana contains several active constituents, including triterpene saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties and are often used to treat various health conditions.
